phases.name: Foundation
phases.goal: "Hello world" on your iPhone via Expo Go
phases.exitCriteria: You can scan a QR code, open the app, log in with email, and see a home screen in your device language.

tasks.title: Initialize Expo project with TypeScript
tasks.notes: Expo SDK 54, React Native 0.81.5, TypeScript 5.9.2 strict, Expo Router v6 with typed routes. scheme: ronex, bundleId: app.ronex.ios

tasks.title: Set up Git repo and push to GitHub
tasks.notes: Repo: github.com/johnnyvanrhijn/Ronex, branch: main, initial commit with full project structure

tasks.title: Create Supabase project
tasks.notes: Supabase project: Ronex, URL: ambnbzvrgvqpiniksklf.supabase.co, .env created with EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY

tasks.title: Install Supabase JS client, configure env vars
tasks.notes: Installed @supabase/supabase-js, @react-native-async-storage/async-storage, react-native-url-polyfill. Client configured in lib/supabase.ts with AsyncStorage auth adapter. .env.example created.

tasks.title: Set up NativeWind (Tailwind for React Native)
tasks.notes: NativeWind 4.2.3 + Tailwind 3.4.19. metro.config.js wraps withNativeWind, global.css imported in _layout.tsx. Theme extend empty for T-006.

tasks.title: Define design tokens (colors, spacing, typography)
tasks.notes: Full token system in tailwind.config.js: semantic colors, Inter font (7 weights via @expo-google-fonts/inter), 6 typography presets, spacing base-4px, border radius presets. lib/theme.ts for programmatic access.

tasks.title: Install and configure Expo Router
tasks.notes: Expo Router v6 was included with SDK 54 tabs template in T-001. File-based routing with (tabs) group already configured.

tasks.title: Install i18next + expo-localization, set up NL + EN structure
tasks.notes: i18next 26 + react-i18next 17 + expo-localization 17. Auto-detects device locale, falls back to EN. Type-safe keys via i18next.d.ts. Translations in i18n/en.json and i18n/nl.json.

tasks.title: Create translations.json with initial strings
tasks.notes: 82 keys added: common, auth, onboarding, navigation, profile, errors, workout. Native NL+EN, TONE.md compliant. Fixed banned 'we' pronoun in existing auth copy.

tasks.title: Implement auth flow (magic link email)
tasks.notes: Magic link via OTP (6-digit code). AuthProvider with session tracking, useProtectedRoute hook for route guarding. (auth)/login.tsx functional but unstyled. Auth keys added to i18n. Deep link scheme ronex:// ready, needs Supabase dashboard redirect URL config.

tasks.title: Design login/signup screens
tasks.notes: 3 premium screens: welcome.tsx (wordmark + dual CTA), login.tsx (email input + magic link), verify.tsx (6-box OTP with auto-advance, paste support, auto-submit, 60s resend cooldown). All NativeWind, all i18n, all accessible. Flow: welcome → login → verify → (tabs).

tasks.title: Basic home screen (placeholder, post-login)
tasks.notes: Dashboard home: time-based greeting, gradient Start Workout CTA, 3-col stats row (placeholders), recent workouts empty state. 4 tabs: Home, Workout, Stats, Profile (with sign-out). All i18n, NativeWind, Ionicons. expo-linear-gradient installed.

tasks.title: Configure eas.json and app.json for builds
tasks.notes: eas.json met development/preview/production profiles. .easignore excludes docs/dashboard/.claude. Placeholders voor EAS project ID, Apple Team ID, ASC app ID — invullen bij eerste build.

tasks.title: Test on physical iPhone via Expo Go
tasks.notes: LAN mode via Expo Go werkt (ngrok/tunnel faalde). Login via 6-digit OTP succesvol, user zit in app op home screen. Observaties: app werkt technisch, maar leeg en visueel nog niet premium. babel.config.js toegevoegd voor NativeWind v4 + Reanimated. Supabase email template staat nog op 8-digit code, moet handmatig naar 6 digits.

tasks.title: Extend design tokens: 3 typographic levels (display-lg, body, small-caps)
tasks.notes: SPEC §14 Rule 5 mandates exactly 3 typographic levels per screen. Add/align tokens: display-lg (28-32px screen titles/primary data), body (16-18px sections/secondary), small-caps (11-12px labels/metadata). Document in lib/theme.ts and tailwind.config.js. Existing 6 typography presets moeten tegen deze 3-niveau hierarchie gemapped worden — cut or collapse where redundant. 2026-04-18: designer produces mockup-proposal FIRST (specs table + mapping + preview), Johnny approves, then implementation + apply to welcome/login/verify screens so change is visible on Expo Go. Phase B shipped. tailwind.config.js + lib/theme.ts updated. Applied to welcome/login/verify/home. 2 documented exceptions: welcome wordmark (52px) + OTP digits (24px) — see docs/design-system.md. Fast Refresh picks up on Johnny's Expo Go instantly.

tasks.title: Refactor tab navigation: 4 tabs (Challenge default, Workout dominant, Stats, Profile)
tasks.notes: SPEC §13 IA restructure (updated 2026-04-18: 4 tabs, not 5). Current: 4 tabs (Home/Workout/Stats/Profile) met Home als default. New: 4 tabs, Challenge tab = default landing (replaces Home), Workout = visually dominant (largest icon, lime accent). Leaderboard is NOT a standalone tab — it lives as a subsection at the top of the Challenge tab (see T-117 scope). Requires renaming (tabs)/index → (tabs)/challenge, tuning Tabs options per SPEC §13. Keep Profile for auth/signout.

tasks.title: Define per-screen UX checklist template (7 questions + 5 states)
tasks.notes: Template created at docs/templates/ux-checklist-template.md. Covers 7 SPEC §14 UX questions (one job / primary action / title / top third / states / no-action path / halve test) + 5 required states table (loading/empty/error/success/edge). PM pastes this into notes of every Phase 1+ designer task. Moved from temp-files/ to docs/templates/ because it's permanent reference, not ephemeral.

phases.name: Exercise Database + Onboarding
phases.goal: User can sign up, complete onboarding, and save profile
phases.exitCriteria: A new user can complete onboarding end-to-end and their profile is in Supabase.

tasks.title: Review profiles schema proposal + beantwoord 4 open vragen
tasks.notes: Design-doc in temp-files/t-101-profiles-schema-proposal.md. 2026-04-18: alle 4 vragen RESOLVED — (1) years_experience vervangen door experience_bucket enum (<1y/1-3y/3-5y/5+y) + UI-mapping in T-109, (2) injuries vervangen door injury_t[] preset enum met 15 values (14 specifiek + 'other'), checkbox-UI in T-112, AI-filtering in T-304, (3) display_name blocklist van 41 woorden (11 impersonatie + 15 NL + 15 EN) via banned_display_names seed-table + word-boundary regex trigger + CHECK length 1-40, (4) gender hernoemd naar biological_sex / biological_sex_t met framed onboarding copy. Extra verbeteringen: timezone-kolom (default Europe/Amsterdam, geseed via Intl.DateTimeFormat bij T-113). Downstream impact: T-108 +0.25u, T-109 +0.25u, T-112 +0.5u, T-113 +0.25u, T-114 +0.5u, T-304 +1u, T-725 +0.5u (totaal +3.25u). T-101 is nu ontblokkeerd.

tasks.title: Design Supabase schema: profiles table + RLS policies
tasks.notes: Built idempotent migration at supabase/migrations/20260418000000_profiles.sql (229 SQL lines). Contents: 7 enums (biological_sex_t, level_t, language_t, usage_type_t, onboarding_type_t, experience_bucket_t, injury_t 15 values) wrapped in do-blocks with duplicate_object guards; banned_display_names lookup table seeded with 41 words (11 impersonation + 15 NL + 15 EN profanity) via on-conflict-do-nothing; check_display_name_allowed() trigger with PostgreSQL word-boundary regex (\m...\M); profiles table with all 17 columns per proposal (id PK→auth.users CASCADE, display_name CHECK 1-40, nullable identity fields until onboarding_completed_at is set, focus_muscle_groups+injuries NOT NULL default '{}', timezone default 'Europe/Amsterdam'); handle_updated_at() trigger on BEFORE UPDATE; handle_new_user() SECURITY DEFINER trigger on auth.users AFTER INSERT for auto-profile-creation; 3 RLS policies on profiles (select/update/insert own via auth.uid()=id), plus deny-all RLS on banned_display_names. Also scaffolded supabase/config.toml stub (project_id placeholder 'ronex', no CLI link yet). No deviations from approved proposal. How to apply: open Supabase dashboard (ambnbzvrgvqpiniksklf) → SQL editor → paste migration file contents → run. Alternatively once CLI is linked: `supabase db push`. Verification queries after apply: `select count(*) from public.banned_display_names;` should return 41; `select count(*) from pg_type where typname in ('biological_sex_t','level_t','language_t','usage_type_t','onboarding_type_t','experience_bucket_t','injury_t');` should return 7; `select tablename from pg_tables where schemaname='public';` should include profiles + banned_display_names; `select policyname from pg_policies where schemaname='public' and tablename='profiles';` should return 3 rows. Also fixed a pre-existing missing comma after the T-101 entry in tasks.json that broke JSON parsing.

tasks.title: Review exercise library proposal + beantwoord 8 open vragen
tasks.notes: Design-doc staat in temp-files/t-103-exercise-library-proposal.md. 2026-04-18: Proposal finaal gepatcht na 4 beslissingen — (1) sumo-deadlift + stiff-leg-deadlift challenge_suitable=false, (2) Q9 equipment availability resolved als optie A (MVP) + SPEC §16 bullet toegevoegd, (3) hip-dominant gender_bias_factor 1.05→1.15 op 8 glute-rows + calibration-note toegevoegd, (4) weighted bodyweight handling sectie toegevoegd (reps_only + optional weight, challenge-scoring negeert added weight). Finale totals: 118 exercises / 88 challenge_suitable / 30 niet / 74%. Q1-Q9 allemaal resolved. Downstream: T-201 +0.5u, T-209 +1.5u, T-304 +0.5u, T-713 +1u (totaal +3.5u, doorgevoerd in tasks.json). T-103 en T-104 zijn nu ontblokkeerd.

tasks.title: Design Supabase schema: exercises + categories tables
tasks.notes: 2026-04-18: Started. Building migration per approved proposal (temp-files/t-103-exercise-library-proposal.md). Scope: 6 new enums (muscle_t, equipment_t, pattern_t, difficulty_t, leverage_t, logging_t) + exercises table with 14 columns (slug-PK id, name, primary_muscle, secondary_muscles[], equipment, movement_pattern, is_compound, is_unilateral, difficulty, gender_bias_factor, bodyweight_leverage, logging_type, challenge_suitable, created_at) + RLS (public read, authenticated, no writes). No separate categories table — muscle_t enum replaces it per proposal. Seed data is T-104/T-105 scope, not T-103. Delivered: supabase/migrations/20260418010000_exercises.sql with all 6 enums (idempotent do-blocks), exercises table (14 NOT NULL columns, slug-PK, gender_bias_factor CHECK [1.00, 2.00], secondary_muscles[] default '{}'), two indexes (primary_muscle full idx, challenge_suitable partial idx WHERE true), and RLS stance per spec (RLS ON + single exercises_select_all policy FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING true, zero write policies — seeds via service_role bypass RLS). Apply via Supabase SQL editor or Management API query endpoint.

tasks.title: Curate canonical exercise list (~100-150 exercises, NL+EN names)
tasks.notes: 2026-04-18: Started. Converting the approved 118-exercise list from temp-files/t-103-exercise-library-proposal.md into data/exercises.json (single source of truth for T-105 seed script). Per proposal decision: English names only (international gym lingua franca), no NL translations needed. Scope: parse 11 markdown tables into JSON array, validate counts per muscle (13/13/13/10/10/5/16/9/10/6/13 = 118), validate challenge_suitable split (88 true / 30 false), validate every id is a valid slug. 2026-04-18 DONE: Delivered data/exercises.json (118 objects, 13 fields each, pretty 2-space indent). All structural checks PASS: length=118, per-muscle counts all match (13/13/13/10/10/5/16/9/10/6/13), 118 unique ids, all ids match slug regex ^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$, all enum values valid against 6 enum sets, all gender_bias_factor values in [1.00, 2.00], every object has exactly the 13 required fields. Edge cases handled: burpee.secondary='full body' prose dropped from source → secondary_muscles=[]; dead-hang primary=forearms per section header; weighted-sit-up logging_type=weight_reps; back-extension=hinge pattern. ANOMALY FOUND in source proposal: §Challenge-suitable analysis summary sentence claims '88 true / 30 false / 74%' but the enumerated exclusion bullet list contains 40 items (14 machine + 16 cable + 10 technical-risk) which matches exactly the per-row ✗/✓ markers in the 11 tables. Actual ratio from per-row markers = 78 true / 40 false (66%). Task spec instructed to use the per-row markers as source of truth, so JSON reflects 78/40. Recommend flagging for Johnny whether to reclassify 10 items (e.g. some cable isolation exercises) back to true to hit the ~80% challenge-suitability target noted in the proposal, OR update proposal summary to match the 78/40 reality. Tracking as non-blocking for T-105 seed (seed script inserts what's in the JSON). 2026-04-18 RECLASSIFICATION (Johnny's call, Option C): flipped 19 exercises false → true to reach 97/21 (82.2%). Flipped: 9 machines (machine-chest-press, pec-deck, leg-press, leg-extension, hack-squat, lying-leg-curl, seated-leg-curl, machine-hip-thrust, hip-abduction-machine — accepted despite brand variance), 5 calves (all calf-raise variants — low stakes), 4 cable (face-pull, pallof-press, cable-crunch, cable-wood-chopper — common movements), 1 barbell (sumo-deadlift — recognized powerlifting variant). Kept FALSE (21): 12 cable isolation (pulley ratio truly varies), 7 technical/injury-risk (pendlay-row, push-press, good-morning, nordic-hamstring-curl, glute-ham-raise, sissy-squat, stiff-leg-deadlift), 2 trivial wrist curls. data/exercises.json updated; temp-files/t-103-exercise-library-proposal.md synchronized (per-row markers, analysis section rebuilt with new categorization, count totals updated to 97/21/82.2%). All enum/id/count validations still PASS. Ready for T-105.

tasks.title: Seed exercises table with canonical list
tasks.notes: 2026-04-18: Started. Building seed migration that inserts all 118 exercises from data/exercises.json into public.exercises. File: supabase/migrations/20260418020000_exercises_seed.sql. Must be idempotent (on conflict do nothing or upsert on id) so re-applying doesn't error. Main agent will apply via Supabase Management API after review. 2026-04-18 DONE: Delivered supabase/migrations/20260418020000_exercises_seed.sql (155 lines, 20968 bytes). Generated programmatically via a throwaway Node script that read data/exercises.json, escaped apostrophes (only occurrence: 'Farmer''s Walk'), emitted muscle_t[] array casts (ARRAY[...]::muscle_t[] for non-empty, ARRAY[]::muscle_t[] for 48 rows with empty secondary_muscles), and formatted gender_bias_factor as 2-decimal numeric to match numeric(3,2). Structure: one multi-row INSERT with 13-column list followed by exactly 118 VALUES tuples (verified: regex match on /^  \(/gm = 118), terminated with ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET updating all 12 non-id columns from EXCLUDED (preserves created_at default). Enum literals are bare quoted strings — Postgres auto-casts inside VALUES. Spot-checks all PASS: (1) first chest 'barbell-bench-press' → ARRAY['triceps','shoulders']::muscle_t[], 'barbell', 1.35, 'weight_reps', true. (2) 'dumbbell-fly' → ARRAY[]::muscle_t[] empty secondary, 'isolation' pattern. (3) 'farmers-walk' → apostrophe correctly doubled to 'Farmer''s Walk', logging_type='time_seconds'. (4) 'push-up' reps_only + bodyweight 'partial'. (5) last row 'weighted-sit-up' has no trailing comma before ON CONFLICT. No 'create_at' field in column list. Helper generator script was deleted after use (no .js artifact in repo). Apply instructions for main agent: POST the file contents to Supabase Management API /pg-meta query endpoint (same channel used for T-101 / T-103); verify with `select count(*) from public.exercises;` → 118, `select count(*) filter (where challenge_suitable) from public.exercises;` → 97, `select logging_type, count(*) from public.exercises group by 1;` → weight_reps 90 / reps_only 22 / time_seconds 6. Re-running the migration is safe (upsert path, created_at unchanged). No gotchas beyond the single apostrophe in 'Farmer''s Walk'.

tasks.title: Welcome screen with 'code' vs 'train' fork
tasks.notes: 2026-04-18: Mockup proposal (docs/mockups/t-106-welcome-fork-proposal.md) approved with UX-optimization nuances: expo-blur glow, ghost secondary CTA, value-prop tagline over generic subtitle, fade-in sequence, version label, clipboard auto-detect, auto-focus, return-key submit, animated button enable transition, error toast component, accessibility labels. Implementation delivered: (1) welcome.tsx rewritten with glow (expo-blur), two-line tagline, Start primary + 'Ik ben uitgedaagd' ghost CTA, 600ms fade-in cascade (glow→wordmark→tagline→buttons, 150ms each with 150ms stagger via reanimated withDelay/withTiming), v0.1.0·beta footer at 40% opacity, haptics on both presses; (2) new enter-code.tsx with back chevron, display-lg title, single full-width TextInput (letter-spacing 1.5, maxLength 8, autoCapitalize characters), 100ms auto-focus, clipboard regex detect /^[A-Z0-9]{3}-?[A-Z0-9]{3,4}$/i with 10s banner + Plakken Pressable, returnKeyType go, 200ms ease-out bg/text color transition via interpolateColor on valid state, stub Alert handler flagged for T-115; (3) new components/Toast.tsx (reanimated translateY+opacity, auto-hide 3s, accessibilityRole alert, accessibilityLiveRegion polite); (4) new deps: expo-blur@15.0.8, expo-haptics@15.0.8, expo-clipboard@8.0.8 (all SDK 54 compatible via npx expo install). Typography rule-5 clean on both screens (welcome: 52px exception + body + small-caps; enter-code: display-lg + body + small-caps). Accessibility: all Pressables labelled and roled, decorative glow hidden, banner announces as alert. Inline strings (flagged for Copy T-114): 'CODE' field label (identical NL/EN), onboarding.enterCodeSubtitle added to i18n inline by Designer (NL: 'Plak 'm hier om mee te doen aan een challenge.' / EN: 'Paste it here to join a challenge.'). Verified via Johnny on physical iPhone after resolving two runtime issues: (a) port conflict on 8081 resolved by killing stale PID 9428, (b) Metro cache staleness causing 'Unable to resolve module ./ExpoClipboard' error fixed with npx expo start --clear. Johnny confirmed no visible regressions post-login (Home/tabs unchanged as expected — welcome flow is pre-auth only). Stale i18n keys onboarding.wantToTrain + onboarding.hasCode can now be deleted; doing that as part of this turnover.

tasks.title: Welcome screen copy (NL + EN)
tasks.notes: 2026-04-18: Started. Running in parallel with T-106 implementation. Johnny provided exact strings (NL+EN) for 8 new i18n keys during T-106 mockup approval. Scope: add keys to i18n/nl.json + i18n/en.json, verify TONE.md compliance, update i18next.d.ts typing if needed. 2026-04-18 DONE: 8 keys added verbatim per Johnny's approval. Files touched: i18n/nl.json (6 new onboarding keys: welcomeHookLine1, welcomeHookLine2, ctaStart, ctaChallenged, enterCodeHint, clipboardBanner; 2 new common keys: paste, errorToast), i18n/en.json (same 8 keys, EN values). {{code}} interpolation preserved in clipboardBanner. TONE.md tone-check: 7/8 pass; 1 FLAG — common.errorToast NL 'Oeps, probeer opnieuw' + EN 'Oops, try again' violate TONE.md banned-words list (NL line 247: 'oei, oeps, helaas'; EN line 254: 'oops, whoops'). Johnny explicitly approved these strings during T-106 mockup review so shipped as-is per Copy-agent instruction 'flag rather than silently edit'. Recommend Johnny confirm intent: either (a) accept the banned-word exception for the generic error-toast (document in TONE.md §evolution), or (b) swap to non-apologetic variant like NL 'Ging mis, probeer opnieuw' / EN 'Didn't work, try again'. i18next.d.ts typing: NO CHANGE. File does not exist in repo — searched repo root, i18n/, types/, and tsconfig.json include-globs; T-008 notes mention 'Type-safe keys via i18next.d.ts' but the file was never created (likely aspirational or removed pre-merge). lib/i18n.ts uses plain resources object without module augmentation, so t('key') is currently typed as returning any. Flag for future typing hardening task but out of T-107 scope. Stale keys for post-T-106 cleanup (flagged here because JSON forbids comments): onboarding.wantToTrain ('Ik wil trainen' / 'I want to train') and onboarding.hasCode ('Ik heb een uitdagingscode' / 'I have a challenge code') become unreferenced once T-106's welcome.tsx rewire lands — delete both from nl.json + en.json after Designer confirms T-106 turnover. Tabs/home/profile sections untouched per task spec.

tasks.title: Full onboarding flow: name + biological_sex screen
tasks.notes: 2026-04-18: estimate 2u → 2.25u (+0.25u). Kolom/enum hernoemd van gender naar biological_sex; screen-framing moet expliciet maken dat dit puur voor challenge-handicap is en nooit gedeeld wordt met andere users (copy zie T-114). Ook display_name input met length 1-40 CHECK (DB-level blocklist-trigger handelt profanity af server-side, client mag generieke foutmelding tonen). 2026-04-18 Started. Scope: first onboarding screen (after verify, before home, shown when profiles.onboarding_completed_at is null). Collect display_name (1-40 chars text) + biological_sex (male/female). Screen 1 of ~5 in full onboarding stack (T-108..T-112). Per memory rule: designer produces mockup proposal first, Johnny approves, then implementation. 2026-04-18 DONE. Implementation summary: (1) new route group app/(onboarding)/ with _layout.tsx (Stack, headerShown:false, gestureEnabled:false on screen 1) and identity.tsx (the screen — SafeArea + KeyboardAvoidingView, auto-focus on mount, animated CTA enable transition mirroring enter-code.tsx, reanimated counter fade-in at 30+ chars or on blocklist error, client-side dev blocklist TODO(T-113)-flagged, success path router.replace('/(tabs)') with TODO(T-109) marker until experience screen exists). (2) Shared progress component at components/onboarding/OnboardingProgress.tsx (5 dots left + small-caps-label right, decorative a11y on dots, progressbar role on container). (3) Draft store at stores/onboardingDraft.ts — zustand with persist+AsyncStorage, key 'ronex-onboarding-draft', full typed shape for T-108..T-112 (displayName, biologicalSex, experienceBucket, usageType, trainingFrequencyPerWeek, preferredSplit, focusMuscleGroups, injuries), per-field setters, clear() for T-113. (4) Temp dev-only 'DEV: Onboarding' Pressable added to Profile tab with TODO(T-113) marker so Johnny can reach the screen before the auth gate exists. (5) 10 i18n placeholder keys added to both nl.json+en.json (onboarding.progressLabel/identityTitle/identitySubtitle/nameLabel/sexLabel/sexMale/sexFemale/sexPrivacyLine1/sexPrivacyLine2/nameNotAvailable) — flagged as placeholders for Copy T-114. Reused existing onboarding.namePlaceholder. (6) SPEC.md §14 Rule 5 extended with 'Typography tokens: casing variants' subsection documenting small-caps-label (uppercase) vs small-caps-helper (sentence-case) — both same size/weight/tracking, both count as single level-3 in the 3-level hierarchy. design-system.md gained a matching 'Casing variants within small-caps' table. (7) Dep: zustand 5.0.12 installed via `npx expo install zustand`. Not touched: auth screens, AuthProvider, T-113 gate (out of scope). Type-check: npx tsc --noEmit exits 0. Dev server: listening on :8081 (PID 29260) after killing stale PID 28416.

tasks.title: Full onboarding flow: experience level screen
tasks.notes: 2026-04-18: estimate 1.5u → 1.75u (+0.25u). DB slaat experience_bucket enum op (<1y / 1-3y / 3-5y / 5+y). UI toont free int-input of picker en mapt client-side naar bucket: 0/null→null, 1→<1y, 2-3→1-3y, 4-5→3-5y, 6+→5+y. Mapping-logica hoort in de screen, niet in de DB. 2026-04-18 Started. Screen 2 van 5 onboarding. Import bestaande OnboardingProgress component (stap 2 van 5), extend onboardingDraft zustand store met experienceBucket setter. Per memory rule: mockup first, Johnny approves, dan implementation. 2026-04-18 Mockup approved (docs/mockups/t-109-experience-proposal.md) + 3 Johnny-answers + 2 extra decisions. DONE. Implementation summary: (1) NEW `app/(onboarding)/experience.tsx` — screen 2/5: title + small-caps-helper subtitle + 4 stacked full-width bucket rows (body font-semibold normal case, border 1→2px + primary on select) + Continue CTA (reanimated interpolateColor mirror of identity.tsx) + subtle skip link below CTA that direct-navigates (writes null, no Continue gate). Write-on-continue pattern like identity.tsx; draft-store hydrates on mount so back-nav preserves selection. (2) NEW `components/onboarding/BackChevron.tsx` — reusable back chevron for screens 2-5: h-10 w-10, hit-slop to 44pt, ionicons chevron-back 24pt in #A3A3A3, haptic selectionAsync + router.back. (3) REFACTORED `components/onboarding/OnboardingProgress.tsx` — added optional `leading?: ReactNode` prop. When present: renders `[leading][dots at ml-2]`. When absent (screen 1 identity.tsx): unchanged `ml-6` indent. JSDoc updated. (4) UPDATED `app/(onboarding)/_layout.tsx` — added `<Stack.Screen name='experience' />` (inherits default gestureEnabled:true for swipe-back). (5) UPDATED `app/(onboarding)/identity.tsx` — Continue now routes to `/(onboarding)/experience` instead of TODO stub to `/(tabs)`. (6) Added 7 i18n placeholder keys to nl.json + en.json (experienceTitle/Subtitle/BucketLt1y/Bucket1to3y/Bucket3to5y/BucketGte5y/SkipLink) — flagged for Copy T-114. Labels deliberately normal-case per Johnny decision 2 (not uppercase small-caps) for consistency with sex-pills. (7) TODO(T-110) forward-reference cast in experience.tsx — typed-routes can't resolve `/(onboarding)/usage-type` until T-110 ships; cast through `as any` with marker. Not touched: stores/onboardingDraft.ts (already complete from T-108), lib/theme.ts, tailwind.config.js, design-system.md, AuthProvider, auth screens, T-113 gate, temp DEV:Onboarding link on Profile tab (still needed for testing). Type-check: npx tsc --noEmit exits 0. Dev server: already running :8081 (PID 29260 from T-108, untouched). Downstream flags: Copy T-114 owns all 7 strings; Backend has nothing new (store setters were T-108).

tasks.title: Full onboarding flow: usage type (loose vs plan)
Full onboarding flow: plan preferences (if plan chosen)
Full onboarding flow: injuries/pain points
tasks.notes: 2026-04-18: estimate 1.5u → 2u (+0.5u). Geen free-form textfield meer — 15 preset injury-checkboxes (injury_t enum: lower_back, upper_back, neck, shoulder_left, shoulder_right, elbow, wrist, knee_left, knee_right, hip, ankle, hamstring, groin, achilles, other). 0-N selecteerbaar, 'other' als catch-all voor post-launch data. Labels NL+EN in T-114.

tasks.title: Onboarding submission: save profile to Supabase
tasks.notes: 2026-04-18: estimate 2u → 2.25u (+0.25u). Bij profile-save ook timezone seeden via Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone (Expo Localization API). Essentieel voor per-user lokale weekly league-reset (SPEC §7.7 maandag 00:00 lokaal, gebruikt in T-725). 2026-04-18 PARTIAL SCOPE pulled forward per Johnny's request: implement (1) AuthProvider route-gate checking profiles.onboarding_completed_at after session, (2) Supabase flush helper writing whatever draft fields exist + timezone + onboarding_completed_at, (3) temporary terminal stub at /(onboarding)/usage-type with 'Klaar voor vandaag' button that flushes + routes home, (4) remove DEV: Onboarding link on Profile tab. Remaining work (full T-113): extend flush to cover T-110/T-111/T-112 fields (usage_type, training_frequency_per_week, preferred_split, focus_muscle_groups, injuries) once those screens ship and replace the stub with the real injuries screen + its Klaar button. 2026-04-18 PARTIAL LANDED: (1) providers/AuthProvider.tsx extended — fetches profiles.onboarding_completed_at for the active user on mount + on auth state change + after verifyOtp, exposes onboardingComplete (null|false|true) + refreshProfile() on the context, uses a fetchTokenRef to drop stale responses on rapid session churn, defensive fallback to `false` if row is missing (shouldn't happen due to handle_new_user trigger). (2) app/_layout.tsx — useProtectedRoute rewritten to branch: no session→/(auth)/welcome, session+onboardingComplete===null→render nothing (loading splash, no route push, prevents flicker), session+false→/(onboarding)/identity, session+true→/(tabs). Also registered (onboarding) group in the Stack screens list. (3) lib/onboarding.ts — new flushOnboardingDraft() helper, reads zustand via getState() outside React, writes display_name+biological_sex+experience_bucket+timezone (Intl.DateTimeFormat resolved)+onboarding_completed_at (new Date().toISOString()), clears draft on success, maps server errors to i18n keys (`nameNotAvailable`/`network`/`generic`), discriminated union return {success:true}|{success:false;error:string}. Does NOT write T-110/T-111/T-112 fields — they stay at table defaults. (4) app/(onboarding)/usage-type.tsx — stub screen: step 3/5 progress + BackChevron, title/subtitle from new i18n keys, single always-enabled Klaar CTA (reanimated interpolateColor same as identity/experience), on press: haptics + flush + refreshProfile + router.replace('/(tabs)'). Toast on error (generic message, never leaks blocklist word). (5) app/(onboarding)/_layout.tsx — registered <Stack.Screen name='usage-type' /> with default gestureEnabled:true. (6) app/(tabs)/profile.tsx — removed DEV: Onboarding Pressable + its useRouter import. (7) i18n/nl.json + i18n/en.json — added onboarding.stubTitle / stubSubtitle / stubDone (Copy T-114 refines). Type-check: npx tsc --noEmit exits 0. No migrations. No schema changes. No commits (per instruction). Not touched: identity.tsx / experience.tsx forward-route (already correct), stores/onboardingDraft.ts. Tonight's flow for Johnny: sign out → welcome → login → verify OTP → lands on /(onboarding)/identity (because onboarding_completed_at is null for both test accounts) → fills name+sex → Continue → experience (pick bucket OR skip) → Continue → usage-type stub → Klaar voor vandaag → profile row updates → gate sees timestamp → /(tabs). Re-login after completion skips onboarding and goes straight to /(tabs).

We delen deze info nooit met andere gebruikers.' / 'This keeps challenges fair. We never share this with other users.'. (2) experience_bucket labels NL+EN voor 4 buckets. (3) 15 injury-labels NL+EN (lower_back t/m other). tasks.title: Minimal onboarding for challenge invitees (name, gender, level)
Test full onboarding flow end-to-end
Challenge-tab landing screen: full §13 content order (scaffold, real data in Phase 7)
tasks.notes: Absorbed T-406 scope on 2026-04-18 (merge: Challenge-tab landing IS the new home screen). SPEC §13 content order top-to-bottom: (1) Leaderboard subsection (league tier + position + top 3-5 preview + 'view full leaderboard' CTA), (2) today's volume positively framed, (3) primary 'Send challenge' CTA, (4) active challenges section, (5) training log preview last 2-3 workouts, (6) next workout suggestion widget, (7) CTA to full history. Phase 1 scope = layout + empty states only, no live data. Real data wiring in Phase 7 (challenges) and Phase 4/5 (workout suggestion). Apply UX checklist (T-017). Replaces the old Home screen built in T-012.

phases.name: Workout Logging (Core)
phases.goal: Offline-first workout logging with PR detection — the app's reason to exist
phases.exitCriteria: You can go to the gym without internet, log a full workout, and see your PR updated when you get home.

tasks.notes: 2026-04-18 (profiles-patch): estimate 2.5u → 3.5u (+1u). Bovenop de bestaande challenge_suitable filter moet de prompt nu ook injury-based exercise filtering toepassen via mapping tussen injury_t enum (15 values uit profiles.injuries) en exercise-tags — bv. lower_back → skip good-mornings/conventional deadlift-variants bij advanced, knee_left/right → skip heavy squat-variants, shoulder_* → skip overhead press-variants, etc. Mapping moet expliciet in de prompt-system-message + test-cases per injury. 'other' injury = geen auto-filter, prompt moet generiek voorzichtig zijn. Historische bump: 2026-04-18 (exercise-library-patch): 2u → 2.5u voor challenge_suitable filter.

tasks.notes: 2026-04-18 (scenarios-patch): estimate 4u → 4.5u (+0.5u). Same-gender early-return conditie toegevoegd: als user_a.biological_sex == user_b.biological_sex → gender_multiplier = 1.0 (skip apply_gender_bias); anders female-user krijgt gender_bias_factor. Scenario A/B/C routing per proposal: A=same-gender (bodyweight+level), B=mixed-gender (gender+bodyweight+level), C=identieke users (geen aanpassing). Zie t-101-profiles-schema-proposal.md §Handicap formula scenarios voor pseudocode. 2026-04-18 (profiles-patch): title + code-refs rename van gender → biological_sex; 0u impact, alleen pure refactor van veldnaam in de formule. Formule-logica (gender_bias_factor × bodyweight_leverage) blijft ongewijzigd. Historische bump: 2026-04-18 (exercise-library-patch): 3u → 4u voor per-exercise gender_bias_factor × bodyweight_leverage adjustment + weighted-bodyweight rule: voor logging_type=reps_only exercises moet de formule workout_sets.weight_kg expliciet negeren bij challenge-scoring (reps × bodyweight-contribution only). Added weight blijft in DB voor PR-tracking maar telt niet mee in challenge-totals.

tasks.notes: 2026-04-18 (scenarios-patch): estimate 4u → 4.5u (+0.5u). 'Aanpassing actief' hint per scenario toegevoegd: same-gender+equal-level+similar-weight → 'Geen aanpassing — vergelijkbare niveaus'; same-gender+different-weight → 'Bodyweight aanpassing'; same-gender+different-level → 'Level aanpassing'; mixed-gender → 'Geslacht + Bodyweight + Level aanpassing'. Dynamic copy-rendering gedreven door T-713 output (multiplier-metadata moet aangeven WELKE factoren niet-1.0 waren). Exacte numerieke multipliers blijven verborgen (mystery > information per SPEC §12), alleen hint-categorie zichtbaar. Zie t-101-profiles-schema-proposal.md §Reveal transparency voor full hint-matrix. Copy-strings landen in T-718.

tasks.notes: 2026-04-18: estimate 3u → 3.5u (+0.5u). Cron moet per-user lokale weekly reset doen op basis van profiles.timezone (IANA tz-string), NIET UTC-based. SPEC §7.7: weekly reset = maandag 00:00 lokale tijd. Implementatie-optie: cron draait elk uur, voor elke user wiens lokale tijd net over maandag 00:00 heen is → rollup triggeren. Alternatief: partitioneren per timezone-bucket. Besluit implementatie-aanpak bij T-725 kickoff.

tasks.title: Analytics instrumentation for rematch behavior (post-MVP data for limit decision)
tasks.notes: Priority upgraded medium → high on 2026-04-18: critical for validating (a) default-landing choice and (b) whether profile-history rematch (deferred in SPEC §16) is actually missed. SPEC §16: 'Rematch limit adjustment (monitor behavior 4 weeks post-launch)'. Track events: rematch_claimed, rematch_chain_length (1/2/3/4+), time_to_rematch (minutes between reveal and claim), rematch_accepted vs abandoned. Use Supabase table 'events' or PostHog if already installed. Minimum viable: log to Supabase with user_id, event_type, metadata JSON. Needed so Johnny can decide after launch whether to cap rematches and whether to bring back the profile-history rematch button.

tasks.title: Empty states copy with viral framing for Challenge landing + Leaderboard
tasks.notes: SPEC §13 explicit empty-state examples: no challenges → 'Challenge someone — get a response within 5 minutes'. No friends → 'Add someone to see them on your leaderboard'. Bronze starter → 'Start where everyone begins. Train to promote'. Must be NL+EN. Explicitly frame emptiness as opportunity, never as lack. Never negative league position framing (see §13 pitfalls).

tasks.title: Full leaderboard sub-screen (reached via 'view full leaderboard' CTA from Challenge tab)
tasks.notes: 2026-04-18: Scope changed — Leaderboard is NOT a standalone tab anymore. SPEC §13 now defines 4 tabs with Leaderboard as a subsection at the top of the Challenge tab. This task builds the FULL leaderboard sub-screen reached via 'View full leaderboard' CTA from the Challenge-tab subsection (the subsection preview itself is part of T-117). This sub-screen shows complete ~20-member league ranking, weekly totals, promotion/degradation zones. Friends toggle removed from scope (Friends tab deferred per SPEC §16). Apply UX checklist (T-017).
